title: podman exec sometimes exits 137
body:
I see this once in a while. Too infrequently to have a good reproducer. Only rootless so far:
```console
$ while :;do bats --filter container /usr/share/podman/test/system/*sdnot*bats || break;done
...mostly works... then:
 ✗ sdnotify : container
   (from function `die' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 413,
    from function `run_podman' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 221,
    in test file /usr/share/podman/test/system/260-sdnotify.bats, line 163)
     `run_podman exec $cid touch /stop' failed with status 137
   $ podman rm --all --force
   $ podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
   $ podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
   quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 9f9ec7f2fdef
   $ podman pull quay.io/libpod/fedora:31
   Trying to pull quay.io/libpod/fedora:31...
   Getting image source signatures
   Copying blob sha256:c28ace6b0c4ae099f6f81091731bdf41d9771d28bad96ae4a3507fe950560930
   Copying config sha256:a7a37f74ff864eec55891b64ad360d07020827486e30a92ea81d16459645b26a
   Writing manifest to image destination
   Storing signatures
   a7a37f74ff864eec55891b64ad360d07020827486e30a92ea81d16459645b26a
   $ podman run -d --sdnotify=container quay.io/libpod/fedora:31 sh -c printenv NOTIFY_SOCKET;echo READY;systemd-notify --ready;while ! test -f /stop;do sleep 0.1;done
   a98e2e8a856772144e2297d4dc3b2d21ffa7ab5ff6088a998168d08add9264f4
   $ podman logs a98e2e8a856772144e2297d4dc3b2d21ffa7ab5ff6088a998168d08add9264f4
   /tmp/podman_bats.HrMDlg/container.sock/notify
   READY
   $ podman logs a98e2e8a856772144e2297d4dc3b2d21ffa7ab5ff6088a998168d08add9264f4
   /tmp/podman_bats.HrMDlg/container.sock/notify
   READY
   $ podman exec a98e2e8a856772144e2297d4dc3b2d21ffa7ab5ff6088a998168d08add9264f4 touch /stop
   [ rc=137 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #| FAIL: exit code is 137; expected 0
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   # [teardown]
   $ podman pod rm --all --force
   $ podman rm --all --force
   a98e2e8a856772144e2297d4dc3b2d21ffa7ab5ff6088a998168d08add9264f4

1 test, 1 failure
```

comments.body: Is this latest code, or a released version?
Sorry. The above is podman-3.3.0-0.20.dev.git599b7d7.fc35 but I've also seen it in f34 (bodhi-testing rpms). I do not see any instances in actual CI.
Alright. I made a pretty substantial change to how exit codes are retrieved in main, but it has not reached F34 yet so that suggests it was not my changes.

https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6094258743541760/html/sys-remote-ubuntu-2104-root-host.log.html
Is this related? Also 137 exit code with podman exec in remote system test.
Interesting - yes, that's the same symptom, and now it's in CI. (FWIW I've started preserving CI logs of flakes, and this is the only instance of the `touch /stop.*137` error I can find).

Another one: [sys podman fedora-33 root host](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6687447762337792/html/sys-podman-fedora-33-root-host.log.html) in #10880
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@flouthoc PTAL
@edsantiago are we experiencing this issue anymore, could you point me to any more such cases. Maybe the root cause is underlying infra running out of memory, I'll try recreating this.

I was able to reproduce the failure using the recipe in comment 0. It took about 45 minutes (I didn't add timestamps, I just checked that window periodically). podman-3.3.0-0.26.rc1.fc35 on 5.13.0-0.rc7.20210625git44db63d1ad8d.55.fc35

I don't see any new instances in CI, though; and I don't regularly monitor bodhi failures.

EDIT: in the (unlikely) case that error log is useful:
```
 ✗ sdnotify : container
   (from function `die' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 431,
    from function `run_podman' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 221,
    in test file /usr/share/podman/test/system/260-sdnotify.bats, line 163)
     `run_podman exec $cid touch /stop' failed with status 137
   $ podman rm --all --force
   $ podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
   $ podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
   quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 9f9ec7f2fdef
   $ podman pull quay.io/libpod/fedora:31
   Trying to pull quay.io/libpod/fedora:31...
   Getting image source signatures
   Copying blob sha256:c28ace6b0c4ae099f6f81091731bdf41d9771d28bad96ae4a3507fe950560930
   Copying blob sha256:c28ace6b0c4ae099f6f81091731bdf41d9771d28bad96ae4a3507fe950560930
   Copying config sha256:a7a37f74ff864eec55891b64ad360d07020827486e30a92ea81d16459645b26a
   Writing manifest to image destination
   Storing signatures
   a7a37f74ff864eec55891b64ad360d07020827486e30a92ea81d16459645b26a
   $ podman run -d --sdnotify=container quay.io/libpod/fedora:31 sh -c printenv NOTIFY_SOCKET;echo READY;systemd-notify --ready;while ! test -f /stop;do sleep 0.1;done
   52cff7db8ddd73a7fef9dc200d21a6e853f40b2e08c68273f6cc12b767b88562
   $ podman logs 52cff7db8ddd73a7fef9dc200d21a6e853f40b2e08c68273f6cc12b767b88562
   /tmp/podman_bats.njUKUq/container.sock/notify
   READY
   $ podman logs 52cff7db8ddd73a7fef9dc200d21a6e853f40b2e08c68273f6cc12b767b88562
   /tmp/podman_bats.njUKUq/container.sock/notify
   READY
   $ podman exec 52cff7db8ddd73a7fef9dc200d21a6e853f40b2e08c68273f6cc12b767b88562 touch /stop
   [ rc=137 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #| FAIL: exit code is 137; expected 0
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   # [teardown]
   $ podman pod rm --all --force
   $ podman rm --all --force
   52cff7db8ddd73a7fef9dc200d21a6e853f40b2e08c68273f6cc12b767b88562
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Exit Code 137 seems to mean that the process is killed by the sigkill. (Or google mentions that it might be the oom-killer, but probably same thing.)
Something is killing the exec process.  Likely candidates would be Podman, Systemd, kernel.
Not sure if this is really frequent in CI as @edsantiago mentioned but I'll try to recreating this.

Seen September 20 in [ubuntu-2104-rootless](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6300526078853120/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2104-rootless-host.log.html). No, it's not frequent, but it's still out there.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

### sys: podman system df - with active containers and volumes
* ubuntu-2104 : sys podman ubuntu-2104 rootless host
  * PR #11655
    * [09-20 09:50](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6300526078853120/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2104-rootless-host.log.html)

### sys: sdnotify : container
* ubuntu-2104 : sys podman ubuntu-2104 rootless host
  * PR #11851
    * [10-18 12:50](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4590700386844672/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2104-rootless-host.log.html)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
My cirrus flake logs show no further instances of this since the 10-18 one. Closing; I'll reopen if it becomes a problem again.

Aaaand, reopening.

### [sys] 235 sdnotify : container
* fedora-35 : sys podman fedora-35 root host
  * PR #12725
    * [12-31 10:12](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5935143033503744/html/sys-podman-fedora-35-root-host.log.html#t--00235)

I tried the reproducer again... it failed, after maybe 1.5 hour, but with a different error:
```
  ...
   $ podman logs 1506cc7fbc6419701fc0b2231e6229477a25796965e3c983b90ade89f5779dce
   /run/notify/notify.sock
   READY
   socat log:
   MAINPID=1852805
   READY=1
   $ podman exec 1506cc7fbc6419701fc0b2231e6229477a25796965e3c983b90ade89f5779dce touch /stop
   Error: writing file `/sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-14904.slice/user@14904.service/user.slice/libpod-1506cc7fbc6419701fc0b2231e6229477a25796965e3c983b90ade89f5779dce.scope/container/cgroup.procs`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
   [ rc=126 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```

Trying to get a faster reproducer (as in, one that doesn't have to pull the `fedora` image), I can't get the `137` error but I get the other two (`failed` and EPERM) very frequently:
```bash
#!/bin/bash

set -e

IMAGE=quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610

i=0
t0=$SECONDS
trap 'echo took: $i iterations, $((SECONDS - t0)) seconds' 0

while :;do
    bin/podman run -d --name foo $IMAGE sh -c 'printenv NOTIFY_SOCKET;echo READY;while ! test -f /stop;do sleep 0.1;done'
    bin/podman logs foo
    bin/podman exec foo touch /stop
    bin/podman wait foo
    bin/podman rm foo
    i=$((i+1))
done
```
```console
$ ./foo.sh
...
a7cf79e112590b6f49aae1304d59789b9bffc761d8de5ba8885cec979941305f
Error: OCI runtime error: error creating systemd unit `libpod-2275a1ca0243378db65593d7b926c6526ed527111da829b89aa914f5ae39160a.scope`: got `failed`
took: 84 iterations, 121 seconds
$ ./foo.sh
...
c4f8a9e5feb1dfec84472843c5235f81021da26785d942316714bbad903ddbe9
READY
Error: writing file `/sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-14904.slice/user@14904.service/user.slice/libpod-c4f8a9e5feb1dfec84472843c5235f81021da26785d942316714bbad903ddbe9.scope/container/cgroup.procs`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
took: 349 iterations, 518 seconds

```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

### [sys] 182 podman kill - test signal handling in containers

$ podman exec 56abbde5727b22309dc2366a7a303bebe54463a84012e2d0de9559a27cca892d touch /stop
[ rc=137 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Seen just a few days ago, this time as [**root** on f35 with netavark](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5105072295116800/html/sys-podman-fedora-35-root-host-netavark.log.html)

Today in [sys remote f35 rootless](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6677190212321280/html/sys-remote-fedora-35-rootless-host.log.html#t--00281)

Just now in [sys podman ubuntu-2110 root](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4997604177084416/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2110-root-host.log.html#t--00287)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@edsantiago Any miracles happen and this disappear?
Sigh. No instances in my logs since the `aarch64` one a month ago.

FWIW, I can always reproduce this error when trying to build a docker image for my [Rust project](https://github.com/sanjayts/zero2prod) on my M1 macbook air.

```
~/zero2prod:master ✓ ➭ podman build --tag zero2prod --file Dockerfile .          
STEP 1/6: FROM rust:1.63.0
WARNING: image platform ({amd64 linux  [] }) does not match the expected platform ({arm64 linux  [] })
STEP 2/6: WORKDIR /app
--> Using cache b334153d1e425c9cd63b195aefb0f7da292f396e92b0f1a8d7afb5f08d703b82
--> b334153d1e4
STEP 3/6: RUN apt update && apt install lld clang -y

--> b672db8d6f2

--> 83824ffa967
STEP 5/6: RUN cargo build --release
    Updating crates.io index
Killed
Error: error building at STEP "RUN cargo build --release": error while running runtime: exit status 137
```
Same error when building with `podman build --platform linux/amd64 --tag zero2prod --file Dockerfile .`

The machine was created using the command `podman machine init --cpus 4 --disk-size 80 --memory 5000`

Latest podman version (4.2.0)

Apologies if this isn't the correct place to post this -- I can create a new issue for this if needed.
@sanjayts I'm going to bet that your issue is a too-tiny podman machine. My (limited) experience with Rust is that it demands a lot of RAM. Can you try bumping up the `--memory` in your VM?
[f36 remote rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6178689502150656/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--00177)

Ugh - seen just now, [f36 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5364495942418432/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00365):
```
not ok 365 sdnotify : play kube - with policies
...
# podman exec --env NOTIFY_SOCKET=/run/notify/notify.sock test_pod-a /usr/bin/systemd-notify --ready
# podman exec test_pod-a /bin/touch /stop
[ rc=137 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```

Hi. I'm not immediately sure if this is related but I am running a set of gitlab self hosted runners in Google cloud and am seeing my gitlab container scanning jobs exit regularly with exit code 137 despite there being no obvious memory constraint on the systems and no invocations of oom-killer in the syslog output. Here are some details of the occurence and of the machine I'm seeing this on:

```
heretic098@runner01:~$ sudo grep 'Job failed: exit code 137' /var/log/syslog
Apr 17 15:57:31 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137
Apr 18 09:29:51 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137
Apr 18 14:13:03 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137
Apr 19 10:38:03 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137
Apr 19 11:20:55 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137
Apr 19 12:09:06 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137
Apr 19 16:12:48 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137
Apr 19 17:50:10 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137

heretic098@runner01:~$ podman --version
podman version 3.4.4

                         1 sse4_2 x2apic movbe popcnt aes xsave avx f16c rdrand hypervisor lahf_lm abm 3dnowprefetch invpcid_single pti ssbd ibrs 
                         ibpb stibp fsgsbase tsc_adjust bmi1 hle avx2 smep bmi2 erms invpcid rtm rdseed adx smap xsaveopt arat md_clear arch_capab

Here is /etc/gitlab/config.toml:
```
concurrent = 20
check_interval = 0
shutdown_timeout = 0

[session_server]
session_timeout = 1800

[[runners]]
  name = "runner01"
  request_concurrency = 12
  url = "https://gitlab.com"
  id = <redacted>
  token = <redacted>
  token_obtained_at = <redacted>
  token_expires_at = 0001-01-01T00:00:00Z
  executor = "docker"
  [runners.custom_build_dir]
    enabled = true
  [runners.cache]
    Type = "gcs"
    Path = "glrunner"
    Shared = true
    [runners.cache.gcs]
      BucketName = <redacted>
  [runners.docker]
    tls_verify = false
    image = "alpine:latest"
    privileged = true
    disable_entrypoint_overwrite = false
    oom_kill_disable = false
    disable_cache = false
    volumes = ["/cache", "/run/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock"]
    shm_size = 0
    host = "unix:///run/podman/podman.sock"
```
I notice this happening consistently at the end of the job when the runner goes to clean up its volumes. It seems to occur right after the volume deletion happens. This is from /var/log/syslog on the runner:
```
Apr 19 17:50:10 runner01 podman[3152928]: 2023-04-19 17:50:10.742911568 +0000 UTC m=+86.413336043 volume remove runner-wpzft1m-project-41460091-co
ncurrent-3-920bb7b3110c23fd-cache-c33bcaa1fd2c77edfc3893b41966cea8
Apr 19 17:50:10 runner01 podman[3152928]: @ - - [19/Apr/2023:17:50:10 +0000] "DELETE /v1.40/volumes/runner-wpzft1m-project-41460091-concurrent-3-9
20bb7b3110c23fd-cache-c33bcaa1fd2c77edfc3893b41966cea8?force=1 HTTP/1.1" 204 0 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
Apr 19 17:50:10 runner01 gitlab-runner[1701482]: #033[0;33mWARNING: Job failed: exit code 137
```
@containers/podman-maintainers since this is now being seen in the Real World, maybe it's a good candidate for Bug Week?

> @containers/podman-maintainers since this is now being seen in the Real World, maybe it's a good candidate for Bug Week?

I created a new `bugweek` label.  Feel free to label more issues.
Cc: @mheon
Since 137 indicates process killed by oom killer. Then I am not sure how we solve this problem.  In testing potentially @Luap99 cleanup of leaks might help.

Assuming the memory reporting in the cirrus tasks are accurate these tests have plenty of free memory so the oom killer
shouldn't do anything here.
Could it be cgroups limiting memory triggering it?  If tools are getting 137 exit codes, is there any way other then OOM Kill?  Google seems to point at it?

Google exit code 137

First response is:
```
Exit code 137 means a container or pod is trying to use more memory than it's allowed. The process gets terminated to prevent memory usage ballooning indefinitely, which could cause your host system to become unstable.
```

The memory thing could totally be a red herring.  Just kill a running container:

```
$ podman run alpine sleep infinity
$ echo $?                         
137                                                  
```

What I see in the journal is that the container dies during the 2nd exec.  I do not have a good explanation yet for why though.  The `kube play` session is run via `time --foreground -v --kill=10 60 podman &` but the [test](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5364495942418432/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00365) runs for a second, so way below 1 minute.

Could this be a very simple race with the `touch /stop` logic? The main container waits for the file then exits. When the container exits it will kill all exec sessions AFAIK. So there is a very tiny window where touch created the file then gets interrupted before it it exits 0, then the main container process sees the file and exits causes podman to kill the exec session.
Very good thinking, @Luap99.  That indeed is a nice explanation of what might be happening.  It has also happened in older versions of this very test and always on the `touch /stop` exec.
Opened https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18319

```
$ podman run alpine sleep infinity
$ echo $?                         
137    
```
Could that be a kernel bug?

man timeout
...
  137  if COMMAND (or timeout itself) is sent the KILL (9) signal (128+9)

Looks like this is expected behavior.  Sadly I never knew this.
A SIGKILL process, exits with 137.
Yes, that's expected.  `128+n` with `kill=9` makes the 137.  I assume an OOM kill is just an ordinary kill which would explain the 137 in the OOM case.

> A SIGKILL process, exits with 137

No it does not. If a process is killed by a signal it has no exit code at all. The parent reaps the child and can get the number of the signal instead of the exit code . It is the shell that returns 128 + number of signal back to us. It is by no means required to do that.
Well podman is catching the exit code and reporting it back via the exit code.

$ podman run --entrypoint sleep alpine 100; echo $?
137

Just wanted to make sure this was not the internal sh doing this.

This looks interesting:

./vendor/github.com/containers/storage/pkg/unshare/unshare_linux.go:						exit(int(waitStatus.Signal()) + 128)
Yes it is common practice to return 128 + signal number but it by no means enforced by the kernel APIs.
Bottom line: 137 should be expected when containers which ignore SIGTERM will exit with 137 exit code.
